_instructions: This is ONE combined file describing an entire Knacktor problem. Fill every field, then bring it back to the repo and run: npm run import-problem <thisFile.json>
Keys starting with _ are notes for you and are ignored by the importer — you may delete them.

ADDING_PROBLEMS.md is the FULL authoring prompt — obey every rule in it and run its §10 self-validation before you output. Fill EVERY line: lineExplanations + syntaxExplanations + narration.byLine for every code line, phaseRules for every executable line.
Supported primitives: array | bar-container | hashmap | stack | queue | tree | linkedList | grid | graph | recursion. See ADDING_PROBLEMS.md §DSL for the mapping fields each one uses.

Expression DSL: + - * / %, comparisons, && || !, ternary, indexing, min/max/len/abs. The cell index in cellStateRules is 'idx' (array/stack/queue) or 'r'/'c' (grid) or 'node_id' (tree/graph). A var's previous-step value is '<name>_prev'.
'solution' is the REAL Python source as a string with \n line breaks. Line numbers are load-bearing — all mapping/narration/explanation keys reference them.
Presets: >=3, >=1 with isEdgeCase true, every preset has the correct expectedOutput, and together they execute every branch of every approach.
visualizationIntent is REQUIRED in every approach — describe what the learner sees at each phase (init, loop-body, match, return). This is how the animation is validated against the algorithm.
title: REPLACE problem title
statement: REPLACE with the FULL exact problem statement. Use \n for line breaks.

approaches.name: REPLACE approach name
approaches.visualizationIntent: REPLACE — describe phase-by-phase what the learner sees. Example: 'init: empty array shown; loop: current element highlighted current, visited cells go grey; match: both indices turn result-green and animation pauses; return: freeze frame with final answer chip.'

lineExplanations.3: REPLACE — what this line does in the algorithm.

syntaxExplanations.2: def declares the method; self is the object, nums is the input.
syntaxExplanations.3: REPLACE — beginner syntax for this line.
